justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Event sourcing correctly identified as appropriate but explicitly not the only option — Option B (append-only audit log) is presented as a lighter alternative. (2) Schema evolution risk identified and explained in detail (upcasting strategy, versioning, schema registry). (3) Snapshotting requirement identified for long-lived aggregates with specific threshold guidance (>50 events). (4) Simpler audit-log alternative presented with SQL schema and honest trade-off comparison. (5) trade_offs include both gains and costs across three decisions. (6) implementation_guidance clearly distinguishes the two approaches with a decision framework based on team experience and timeline.

observations.text: Excellent treatment of the simpler alternative — includes concrete SQL schema, honest 80/20 assessment, and clear decision criteria for choosing between the two approaches
Schema evolution risk treated as Critical severity with actionable mitigation (upcasting, versioning, schema registry)
Snapshotting addressed both as an architectural component and in pitfalls-to-avoid section
Goes beyond rubric requirements with GDPR/crypto-shredding consideration, which is highly relevant for a financial domain with audit requirements
Domain events are well-designed with who/what/when captured, demonstrating deep understanding of the audit use case
Honest confidence assessment that flags team experience as the biggest risk factor
Output is extremely verbose for an architecture proposal — could be more concise while hitting the same points
